2606.30190.title: Few-Shot Domain Incremental Learning via Continual Vision-Language Consolidation
2606.30190.paper_summary: Existing domain-incremental learning (DIL) strategies call for massive amounts of data to adapt to new domains and suffer from the overfitting problem in the case of data scarcity. This paper puts forward a relatively uncharted problem, namely, few-shot domain incremental learning (FSDIL), taking into account the problem of extreme data shortages in the realm of DIL. A novel algorithm, namely Continual Vision-Language Consolidation (CVLC), is proposed to address the FSDIL problem, where the key idea lies in the concept of latent space reservation in the base domain coupled with dual coalescent projection (DCP) as a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method. First, the vision prototype is calibrated while multiple templates and synonyms are generated via LLMs to induce the language prototype. The vision and language prototypes are fused. Adaptation to never-ending arrivals of new domains is done by the DCP technique, fine-tuned in such a way to prepare the model to unseen domains via latent-space reservations committed in the base domain. CVLC is structured under shared and domain-specific components to combine general knowledge and domain-specific details. The advantage of our approach is demonstrated through a range of benchmark problems and comparisons with prior arts, in which CVLC outperforms them by up to a 16% gap. Our codes are shared publicly in https://github.com/Naeem-Paeedeh/CVLC .
2606.30190.paper_summary_zh: 現有的領域增量學習（DIL）策略需要大量數據來適應新領域，並且在數據稀缺的情況下會遭遇過擬合問題。本文提出了一個相對未被探索的問題，即少量樣本領域增量學習（FSDIL），考慮到DIL領域中極端數據短缺的問題。為了解決FSDIL問題，提出了一種新穎的算法，即持續視覺-語言整合（CVLC），其關鍵思想在於在基礎領域中保留潛在空間的概念，並結合雙重融合投影（DCP）作為一種參數高效的微調方法。首先，視覺原型被校準，同時通過LLMs生成多個模板和同義詞以引導語言原型。視覺和語言原型被融合。對於不斷到來的新領域的適應是通過DCP技術來實現的，微調方式旨在通過在基礎領域中保留的潛在空間來準備模型面對未見的領域。CVLC在共享和領域特定組件下結構化，以結合一般知識和領域特定細節。我們的方法的優勢通過一系列基準問題和與先前技術的比較得以展示，其中CVLC的表現超過了它們，差距達到16%。我們的代碼已公開分享於 https://github.com/Naeem-Paeedeh/CVLC 。
2606.30190.author: Naeem Paeedeh et.al.
2606.30190.authors: Naeem Paeedeh, Mahardhika Pratama, Wolfgang Mayer, Mukesh Prasad, Weiping Ding, Yew-Soon Ong
